question: Will CPI rise more than 0.8% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has been officially released by the BLS on April 10, 2026. The Consumer Price Index increased 0.3% on a seasonally adjusted basis in March 2026, which is significantly below the 0.8% threshold required for a YES resolution. Since 0.3% is not more than 0.8%, this market should resolve to NO with 100% certainty.
key_factors: Official BLS data shows March 2026 CPI increased only 0.3% seasonally adjusted
0.3% is substantially below the 0.8% threshold required for YES resolution
Market resolution criteria explicitly requires MORE than 0.8% for YES outcome
Data has been officially released on scheduled date of April 10, 2026
risks: Potential data revisions by BLS in future months (though historically rare for headline CPI)
Misinterpretation of resolution criteria (though clearly states 'more than 0.8%')
Possible confusion between seasonally adjusted vs non-seasonally adjusted figures
